_meta.note: Categories and mechanics follow Pandora’s Box (Revised) first; Hero and other tier books where PB cross-references them. Example rows are paraphrased samples for chargen — use your PDFs for full write-ups.
_meta.relicDetails: On `birthrightType: "relic"`, optional `relicDetails` holds: `rating` (1–5 Relic dots), `tagIds` (from data/tags.json), `purviewId` + `purviewRating` (1–5) for a linked Purview hook, `evocation` and `motifsAndTags` text — confirm Imbue and Boons with Pandora’s Box.
_meta.creatureDetails: On `birthrightType: "creature"`, optional `creatureDetails.tagIds` lists master tags (appliesTo includes "creature") for Flairs / fiction.
_meta.introduction: Birthrights are divine gifts and assets: Relics, Guides, Followers, Cults, Creatures, and more. At Hero tier, this wizard uses **seven** Birthright points total on the Birthrights step (Visitation / Hero ch. 3). Finishing Touches at Hero only offers **two extra Knacks** here — not a second Birthright pool stacked on top of the seven.
_meta.pointBudgets: Birthright **points** cap: **4** for Mortal / Origin (Finishing only) and Sorcerer Finishing; **7** for Hero (Birthrights step only); **11** for Demigod / God (combined Birthrights + Finishing Birthright points in this wizard — confirm at your table). Each pick lists its point cost.

typesTable.what: Myth-imbued gear — weapons, jewelry, vehicles; often unlocks Purviews or Knacks tied to the object.
Teacher, ancestor shade, spirit ally, or messenger who offers knowledge and stunts.
Mortal or minor mythic assistants, specialists, or a small retinue.
Companion beast, mount, or guardian entity with its own Health and Knacks/Flairs.
Worshippers, shrines, and rites that support your Legend and provide narrative leverage.

relic.name: Relic (blank template)
relic.description: Design any mythic object with your Storyguide; set dots, Purviews, and Knacks per Pandora’s Box.
relic.mechanicalEffects: Spend Birthright points at chargen per tier book; track tags, Knacks, and Purview access attached to the Relic.

creature.name: Creature (blank template)
creature.description: Generic companion slot — stat Health, Defense, and Knacks/Flairs with the table.
creature.mechanicalEffects: Use Creature Birthright rules for dots, Health, Defense, and Knacks/Flairs.

follower.name: Follower (blank template)
follower.description: Name your retinue or specialist; define Skills and role with the book’s Follower rules.
follower.mechanicalEffects: Follower Birthright rules in Pandora’s Box / Hero.

cult.name: Cult (blank template)
cult.description: Shrines, faithful, and rites — scope with your Storyguide.
cult.mechanicalEffects: Cult Birthright rules in later tier books; not emphasized at Origin.

guide.name: Guide (blank template)
guide.description: Any guiding spirit or mentor archetype.
guide.mechanicalEffects: Guide rules in Pandora’s Box — Enhancement, narrative access, and Knacks per rating.

exRelicWeapon.name: Example: heirloom blade (Relic)
exRelicWeapon.description: Family sword rumoured to cut both steel and oath-breakers; motif of honour and lineage.
exRelicWeapon.mechanicalEffects: Often 2–3 dots: base Relic + Purview or Knack access; add Tags and Motifs in play.

exRelicJewelry.name: Example: ring of the storm (Relic)
exRelicJewelry.description: Sky-metal band that hums when danger nears; channels weather omens.
exRelicJewelry.mechanicalEffects: Higher-dot Relics stack multiple Purview/Boon hooks — price with your SG.

exGuideAncestor.name: Example: grandmother’s shade (Guide)
exGuideAncestor.description: Appears in smoke and old photographs to warn or teach family lore.
exGuideAncestor.mechanicalEffects: Guides commonly grant Enhancement to relevant rolls and story access to Underworld/ghost lore.

exGuideRaven.name: Example: talking raven courier (Guide)
exGuideRaven.description: Odin-style messenger — sarcastic, well-travelled, poor sense of volume.
exGuideRaven.mechanicalEffects: Low-dot Guide: narrow Enhancement + scouting scenes.

exFollowerDriver.name: Example: trusted driver / fixer (Follower)
exFollowerDriver.description: Knows every back alley, owes you more than money, carries a go-bag for divine nonsense.
exFollowerDriver.mechanicalEffects: Follower dots buy Skill ratings, Assets, and small teams per Pandora’s Box.

exFollowerScholar.name: Example: occult archivist (Follower)
exFollowerScholar.description: Runs a basement reading room full of banned bestiaries and polite warnings.
exFollowerScholar.mechanicalEffects: Two-dot follower can cover Academics/Occult support and safe-house scenes.

exCreatureHorse.name: Example: cloud-tread mount (Creature)
exCreatureHorse.description: Sleipnir-inspired steed — too clever, too fast, hates toll booths.
exCreatureHorse.mechanicalEffects: Creatures use separate Health tracks and Knack-like Flairs; higher dots = more Scale.

exCreatureHound.name: Example: iron-jawed guardian hound (Creature)
exCreatureHound.description: Bred to harry Titanspawn; whines when you skip walkies.
exCreatureHound.mechanicalEffects: Mid-tier companion: combat Assist, chase scenes, intimidation.

exCultStreetShrine.name: Example: alley shrine network (Cult)
exCultStreetShrine.description: Candles, graffiti sigils, and food offerings left where the homeless sleep safe.
exCultStreetShrine.mechanicalEffects: Cults grant Reach, rites, and social Infrastructure — see Hero / Pandora’s Box.

exCultOnline.name: Example: cryptid livestream cult (Cult)
exCultOnline.description: Fans who ‘donate’ Legend through memes and midnight watch parties.
exCultOnline.mechanicalEffects: Small cult: narrative leverage, rumor control, minor Resources.
